scope: Cursor plus provider-reported GLM 5.2 High rows only; no new model calls were made and the immutable original result artifact was not modified.

adjudication_method.classification: post hoc and not blind: the recovery rule and transport audit were applied after the original outputs were available

accepted: The complete trimmed output is one JSON object that parses and satisfies the submission schema.
Otherwise, the output contains exactly one unambiguous fenced JSON object; that object parses and satisfies the complete submission schema. Surrounding prose is ignored.
rejected: Zero or multiple fenced JSON candidates.
A candidate that is malformed or schema-incomplete.
Any recovery based on scanning arbitrary prose for balanced braces or choosing among competing objects.
submission_rule.reason: Cursor's terminal result is full assistant text. The original parser required either pure JSON or a JSON fence covering the entire output, so it rejected four otherwise unambiguous schema-complete fenced submissions solely because short prose appeared outside the fence.

private_deterministic_replay.purpose: Reconstruct the original public state/action trajectory and score only the conservatively recovered final submission without publishing private episode state or raw transcripts.
private_deterministic_replay.result: Every recorded public-call result hash matched its deterministic replay for every episode.
private_deterministic_replay.counting_note: Replay match counts refer to transcript public-call result hashes. The scorecard tool_calls field is a separate evaluator metric and is not expected to use the same count.
private_deterministic_replay.scorer: the exact evaluator at the source execution commit

adjudication_method.fixed_denominator: All five planned Cursor assignments remain in the denominator. The malformed episode remains a zero.

transport_audit.finding: The flagged records were normal MCP completion records that omitted provider and tool arguments. Every concrete invocation record named an allowed epiagent tool. Treating an identity-less completion as a new forbidden invocation produced the false flags.
transport_audit.required_evaluator_fix: Correlate starts and completions by call ID. An identity-less completion attached to an authorized start inherits that start's identity; an uncorrelated or unknown-shape event is not proof of a forbidden call, but must fail integrity as an unverifiable transport trace.

persistence_audit.finding: Cursor chat databases remained under ~/.cursor/chats after the temporary CURSOR_DATA_DIR was deleted.
persistence_audit.observed_content_scope: Public episode observations, public tool traffic, and submitted assistant output persisted globally; the audit found no latent simulator parameters or ground-truth state in that persisted material.
persistence_audit.risk: Host-global retention can create cross-episode state and contamination even when the per-assignment data directory is temporary.
persistence_audit.score_treatment: No post-hoc score adjustment, because deterministic replay matched the recorded public-call results; this limitation independently prevents hermetic or leaderboard claims.
persistence_audit.required_before_future_cursor_panel: Use a disposable HOME or stronger OS/container boundary, verify no state is written outside it, and destroy the snapshot after each assignment.

adjudicated_summary.score_treatment: development-only post-hoc adjudication; do not substitute for a prospectively precommitted rerun

episodes.transport_adjudication: original unauthorized-tool flag cleared as an identity-less completion-record false positive
original unauthorized-tool flag cleared as an identity-less completion-record false positive

interpretation: The published 0/5 Cursor validity result was mostly a harness parsing artifact, not evidence that four of the five assistant outputs lacked benchmark submissions.
The corrected development-only fixed-denominator result is 4/5 valid, mean 44.748, and median 55.002.
All four recovered submissions still scored zero on response utility and incurred the same three substantive closed-loop violations; the adjudication does not erase those scientific shortcomings.
This artifact preserves the original zero-result artifact and records a post-hoc correction. It is not a retrospective replacement for a prospectively precommitted, isolated rerun.
limitations: The parser rule was not precommitted and was selected after the original outputs were available.
The deterministic replay used private episode state and has not been independently reproduced from a public replay bundle.
Cursor's model identity is provider-reported and not independently signed.
The original run was host-networked and non-hermetic; ~/.cursor/chats persisted public episode state outside the requested temporary data directory.
The panel contains one synthetic development episode per causal family and is too small for uncertainty estimates or scientific ranking.

hash_specification.self_hash_rule: Remove the adjudication_sha256 member, canonicalize the remaining document, and hash those bytes.
hash_specification.original_public_row_hash_rule: Canonicalize the complete matching Cursor result row from the immutable source artifact with the same canonicalization and hash those bytes.
